title: NAZAROV CYCLIZATION
summary: Overview page for the Nazarov cyclization describing acid- or Lewis-acid-promoted electrocyclization of divinyl ketones through pentadienyl cation intermediates to cyclopentenones.
notes: Key concepts: divinyl ketone precursors, 4π conrotatory electrocyclization, pentadienyl cation intermediates, control by beta-substituents and silicon directing groups, and stereospecific ring closure to cyclopentenones.

title: NAZAROV CYCLIZATION
summary: Application page for the Nazarov cyclization featuring (+)-trichodiene, (+)-silphinene, (+)-desepoxy-4,5-didehydromethylenomycin A methyl ester, and inverto-yuehchukene assembled through electrocyclic cyclopentenone formation.
notes: Examples emphasize stereocontrolled Nazarov ring closures in terpenoid and indole frameworks, including silicon-directed variants and late-stage formation of fused five-membered rings.

title: NEBER REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Neber rearrangement describing base-induced conversion of O-acylated ketoximes into alpha-amino ketones through azirine intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: preparation of O-acyl ketoximes from oximes, rearrangement under alkoxide base, competing concerted and nitrene pathways, formation of 2H-azirines, and utility for alpha-amino ketone synthesis.

title: NEBER REARRANGEMENT
summary: Application page for the Neber rearrangement featuring a β3 adrenergic receptor agonist intermediate, 3-aminopiperidine scaffolds, DL-vinylglycine, and optically active 3-amino-2H-azirines prepared through oxime activation and rearrangement.
notes: Examples emphasize one-pot oxime formation/tosylation/rearrangement sequences, regioselective aminopiperidine synthesis, and access to amino acid or azirine building blocks.

title: NEF REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Nef reaction describing conversion of nitronate salts or nitroalkanes into carbonyl compounds under acidic or oxidative conditions.
notes: Key concepts: acid-dependent hydrolysis of nitronate salts, conversion of primary and secondary nitro compounds into aldehydes or ketones, access to oximes and carbonyl products, and reductive or oxidative variants of the Nef manifold.

title: NEF REACTION
summary: Application page for the Nef reaction featuring the spiroketal core of the γ-rubromycins, spiropyrrostatin B, (-)-cyclophellitol, and BCX-1812 prepared through nitro-to-carbonyl conversion.
notes: Examples emphasize tandem Henry/Nef sequences, in situ reduction to aldehyde oximes followed by hydrolysis, and strategic oxidation of nitro cyclitols to carboxylic-acid functionality.

title: NEGISHI CROSS-COUPLING
summary: Overview page for the Negishi cross-coupling describing Pd- or Ni-catalyzed coupling of organozinc reagents with aryl, vinyl, allyl, or alkynyl electrophiles.
notes: Key concepts: organozinc nucleophiles generated by direct insertion or transmetalation, broad functional-group tolerance relative to Grignard reagents, stereoretentive cross-coupling, and catalytic cycles involving oxidative addition, transmetalation, and reductive elimination.

title: NEGISHI CROSS-COUPLING
summary: Application page for the Negishi cross-coupling featuring caerulomycin C, (-)-motuporin fragments, and (+)-amphidinolide J assembled through coupling of organozinc subunits with aryl or vinyl halides.
notes: Examples emphasize late-stage coupling in heteroaryl systems, Schwartz-hydrozirconation/transmetalation sequences, and convergent assembly of stereodefined diene-containing natural-product fragments.

title: NENITZESCU INDOLE SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the Nenitzescu indole synthesis describing condensation of p-benzoquinones with β-aminocrotonates or related enamine-type partners to furnish 5-hydroxyindoles.
notes: Key concepts: p-benzoquinone electrophiles, β-aminocrotonate or β-amino-α,β-unsaturated carbonyl partners, formation of substituted 5-hydroxyindoles, regioselectivity effects of quinone substitution, and a mechanistic pathway involving Michael addition followed by cyclization.

title: NENITZESCU INDOLE SYNTHESIS
summary: Application page for the Nenitzescu indole synthesis featuring EO 9, LY311727, polymer-supported 5-hydroxyindole carboxamides, and substituted tetrahydrocarbolines assembled from benzoquinones and amino/enol partners.
notes: Examples emphasize concise access to highly functionalized hydroxyindoles for bioreductive alkylating agents, phospholipase A2 inhibitors, solid-phase synthesis, and Lewis-acid-directed variants that extend the scope to tetrahydrocarbolines.
